/- A linear operator T between two normed spaces is said to be of restricted weak type
  (p, q) if its behavior on simple functions satisfies certain conditions
  related to the restricted weak L^p and L^q spaces. This concept is closely
  related to the notion of operators that are bounded on L^p and L^q spaces
  and their duals. -/
noncomputable def HasLorentzType {α : Type*} {β : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup α] [NormedAddCommGroup β]
    {T : α → β} {p q : ℝ≥0∞} {μ : Measure α} {ν : Measure β}
    (c : ℝ≥0) (hc : 0 < c) :
    ∀ (f : α → ℝ), (LocallyIntegrable f μ) →
      eLpNorm (T f) 1 ν ≤ c * eLorentzNorm f p q μ * (ν.univ).toReal ^ (1 / p : ℝ) := by
  sorry

/- The weak continuity assumption neede for `HasRestrictedWeakType.hasLorentzType_helper`. -/
def WeaklyContinuous [TopologicalSpace ε] [ENorm ε] [ENorm ε'] [SupSet ε] [Preorder ε] (T : (α → ε) → (α' → ε')) (p : ℝ≥0∞) (μ : Measure α) (ν : Measure α') : Prop :=
  ∀ {fs : ℕ → SimpleFunc α ε} (_ : Monotone fs),
  let f := fun x ↦ ⨆ n, (fs n) x;

    eLpNorm (T f) 1 (ν.restrict G) ≤ Filter.limsup (fun n ↦ eLpNorm (T ⇑(fs n)) 1 (ν.restrict G)) Filter.atTop
